sense.definition: a set of large folded sheets of paper with lots of stories, pictures, and information printed on them about things like sport, politic, etc., usually issued daily or weekly

examples.x: I enjoy doing the crossword puzzle in the newspaper to challenge my brain.
I like to read the newspaper with a cup of coffee to start my day.
I use the newspaper as a source for my research papers because it has reliable information.
The newspaper has a column where readers can write in and ask for advice.
The newspaper has an entertainment section with movie reviews and celebrity news.

sense.definition: a colorful thin book that has news, pictures, and stories about different things like fashion, sports, and animals, usually issued weekly or monthly

examples.x: I often read parenting magazines to get advice on raising my children.
I subscribe to a science magazine that keeps me updated on scientific discoveries.
My mom subscribes to a cooking magazine, and we often try new recipes from it.
The magazine features interviews with musicians and reviews of new albums.
The library has a wide selection of magazines on different subjects.

sense.definition: a regularly distributed publication that provides news, updates, and information about a specific topic, organization, or community to its subscribers

examples.x: I receive a weekly newsletter from my favorite online store with updates on new products and discounts.
She signed up for the neighborhood newsletter to learn about upcoming community events.
The company sends out a newsletter every month to keep employees informed about the latest news and events.
The newsletter included important updates about changes in the company's policies.
I always look forward to reading the monthly newsletter from the local library for book recommendations.

sense.definition: a list of items in a particular category, especially one systematically arranged
a small book consisting of a few sheets, typically of paper or card, stapled or bound together, often containing information on a specific subject
a book that provides instructions or guidance for operating, maintaining, repairing, or assembling a product or system
a publication, especially about a technical subject, that is produced regularly

examples.x: My local library carries a wide selection of periodicals, including magazines, journals, and newspapers.
I enjoy reading the latest issue of my favorite periodical over breakfast every morning.
As a subscriber to several periodicals, I look forward to receiving each new issue in the mail.

sense.definition: a magazine that is written by and intended for fans of a musician, group, etc.
a small card that contains contact information for a person or company, used to share and promote professional connections

examples.x: She handed out her business card at the networking event.
The business card included his name, phone number, and email address.
He forgot to bring his business cards to the meeting.
The company’s logo stood out on the business card design.
She kept his business card to contact him later about the job opportunity.

sense.definition: a booklet with a few pages and paper covers containing information or advertisement that is intended for free distribution
a specialized magazine or periodical that provides industry-specific news, analysis, and information to professionals within a particular field or sector

examples.x: The trade publication featured an article about new software tools for the tech industry.
I saw an advertisement for a conference in a trade publication that I read for my job.
He wrote an article for a trade publication on best practices in healthcare management.
The trade publication has a section dedicated to interviews with industry leaders.
She subscribed to a trade publication to stay updated on the latest marketing trends.

sense.definition: a book typically small, with information, images, and details about a product, service, organization, or event

examples.x: The hotel provided a colorful brochure outlining all the amenities and local attractions for guests to explore.
She picked up a brochure at the travel agency to learn more about the exotic destinations they offered.
The university sent out brochures to prospective students detailing the academic programs and campus facilities.
After browsing the brochure, he decided to sign up for the cooking class advertised inside.
The company's new product brochure showcased stunning images and comprehensive specifications to attract potential buyers.

sense.definition: a small book with a paper cover giving information about a particular subject

examples.x: The health clinic distributed pamphlets about nutrition and exercise to promote healthy lifestyle habits.
Tourists visiting the city could pick up pamphlets at the information center to learn about local attractions and events.
The environmental organization produced pamphlets to raise awareness about conservation efforts and ways individuals can help protect the planet.
Before the workshop, attendees received pamphlets outlining the agenda and topics to be covered during the session.
The political candidate's campaign team handed out pamphlets outlining their platform and proposed policies to potential voters.

sense.definition: a book produced annually that highlights events, activities, and people from a specific period within a year

examples.x: Each year, the company publishes a yearbook showcasing the company's accomplishments, milestones, and employee achievements.
The city's historical society produces a yearbook documenting significant events, developments, and changes in the community over the past year.
The environmental organization releases an annual yearbook detailing conservation efforts, environmental challenges, and progress made in protecting natural resources.
The local photography club publishes a yearbook showcasing members' best photos, group outings, and photography tips from the past year.
The neighborhood association releases a yearbook highlighting community events, volunteer efforts, and initiatives aimed at improving the neighborhood.
